label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the article to find out how the device works. The first paragraph says the device is designed to get rid of pollutants from Beijing's smog skies. Then it mentions that an electromagnetic field will pull particles in the smog to the ground where they can be cleaned. So the method involves using an electromagnetic field to pull the particles down. The next part talks about Daan Roosegaarde, the artist, explaining it like a balloon with static that makes your hair go towards it. The analogy is that the smog particles are pulled toward the device similarly. The device pulls the particles down to the ground. The question asks how the device works. The options are: (A) using more masks, (B) using an electromagnetic field to pull pollutants to the ground, (C) using a balloon with static to absorb hair, (D) creating clean air and releasing it. Option B matches the description in the article. Option C
